overview: From 2026-05-22 to 2026-05-23, elizaos/eliza had 5 new PRs (10 merged), 0 new issues, and 7 active contributors.

topPRs.title: fix(chip): keep source-only riscv checks clean
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- add a `--report` override to `scripts/check_linux_external_bsp.py` so callers can write host-local status under `build/reports`
- route the minimum-Linux nested external-BSP check to `build/reports/linux-external-bsp-status.json` instead of dirtying tracked release evidence under `docs/evidence/...`
- make optional Hypothesis/CAD/rendering chip tests skip and typecheck cleanly whether optional Python deps are absent locally or present in the Docker image
- add regression coverage proving custom external-BSP reports do not mutate tracked evidence
- normalize existing `packages/chip` AI-EDA/TEE script ruff/format/mypy debt so the Docker regression gate can run the chip lint/typecheck target cleanly
- keep the E1 IOMMU/cache/BPU RTL clean under the `eliza-soc-tools` Verilator gates by removing inferred combinational latches and Verilator-unsupported reset/allocation loop patterns
- include `e1_soc_integrated_tb_test_cross_domain_interfaces` in the release cocotb evidence manifest and make `evidence-regression-test` produce the synthesis/formal/verilator/cocotb artifacts that `pipeline_check.py` requires
- keep the prototype status dashboard checker stable when CI has generated transient synthesis/cocotb/verilator/formal/qemu artifacts but the tracked dashboard intentionally remains a conservative source-tree baseline
- merge latest `origin/develop` (`7bddb5d46a`) into this branch and typecheck the newly merged AI-EDA helper scripts so the branch is current with Shaw's latest RISC-V/chip work
- remove a duplicate `pluginWorkerRuntimeSrc` declaration in `packages/app-core/vitest.config.ts` from the merged develop tree so Release Workflow Contract can parse the app-core Vitest config

## Scope

RISC-V / E1 chip source-only and Docker-gate validation hygiene. This does not touch USB/Tails work, does not claim board/silicon evidence, and does not promote the Debian riscv64 image.

The broad script-format commit is intentionally mechanical and separate from the functional report-routing fix: CI reached `docker-regression` and failed on pre-existing chip-wide ruff/format debt, so this PR includes the minimum chip script normalization needed for that gate.

The RTL commits are gate-driven: after lint/typecheck passed in Docker, the checked Verilator targets exposed real E1 IOMMU/cache/BPU RTL issues. The fixes keep behavior equivalent while making the source acceptable to the checked Verilator version and the cross-domain cocotb integration target.

The dashboard commit is similarly gate-driven: `ci-fast` and evidence-regression runs create `build/**` evidence before `prototype-status-dashboard-check`, while the tracked dashboard documents the conservative source-tree baseline. The checker now tolerates that volatile generated-artifact drift only for known generated-output subsystems and still rejects stale nonvolatile dashboard rows.

The app-core Vitest change is a minimal CI-unblocker from the merged latest develop tree: Release Workflow Contract failed before tests could run because the config declared the same const twice.

## Validation

Local/source checks:

- `PATH=/tmp/eliza-riscv-ruff-venv/bin:$PATH make -C packages/chip lint` -> ruff lint/format, JSON syntax, and git whitespace passed; local shellcheck/yamllint reported blocked because those tools are not installed locally
- `PATH=/tmp/eliza-riscv-ruff-venv/bin:$PATH make -C packages/chip typecheck` -> mypy, Python syntax, platform contract schema, project plan schema, and software BSP schema passed; external BSP promotion evidence remains correctly reported as `BLOCKED`
- same `make -C packages/chip typecheck` rerun after installing `hypothesis` and `cadquery` into the temp venv to match the Docker optional-dependency branch
- `python3 -m compileall -q packages/chip/scripts packages/chip/benchmarks/parsers packages/chip/compiler/runtime packages/os/linux/variants/elizaos-debian-riscv64/scripts`
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/chip/scripts -p 'test_*.py'` -> 182 tests, OK, 5 skipped
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/chip/benchmarks/parsers/tests -p 'test_*.py'` -> 16 tests, OK, 1 skipped
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/os/linux/variants/elizaos-debian-riscv64/scripts -p 'test_*.py'` -> 37 tests, OK, 16 skipped
- focused Chipyard/Renode/QEMU status tests passed:
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_preflight.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_linux_smoke.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_smoke_robustness.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_renode_status.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_qemu_smoke_status.py`
- dashboard validation:
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/test_check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- cross-domain manifest regression:
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/test_cocotb_pipeline_targets.py`
  - `python3 -m compileall -q packages/chip/scripts/check_cocotb_results.py packages/chip/scripts/test_cocotb_pipeline_targets.py`

Docker validation from `packages/chip`:

- `docker build -t eliza-soc-tools .`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make rtl-check`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools python3 scripts/test_check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools python3 scripts/check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck prototype-status-dashboard-check`
- after merging latest `origin/develop`: `docker run --rm -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work/packages/chip eliza-soc-tools make evidence-regression-test`
  - synthesis, Verilator smoke, NPU GEMM smoke, Yosys formal fallback manifest, cocotb chip/NPU/tiny CPU, and cross-domain integration cocotb all passed
  - `e1_soc_integrated_tb_test_cross_domain_interfaces`: 11 tests, 11 passed
  - strict release gate regression passed
  - final line: `evidence regression tests complete`
- after merging latest `origin/develop`: `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work/packages/chip eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck` passed
- app-core Vitest config fix:
  - `git diff --check` passed
  - `rg -n "const pluginWorkerRuntimeSrc|plugin-worker-runtime" packages/app-core/vitest.config.ts` shows one declaration and the existing aliases
  - full local Release Workflow Contract was not run because this sparse RISC-V worktree does not have the app-core dependency tree installed; the CI failure was a parse-time duplicate declaration and this patch removes that duplicate directly
- final hygiene:
  - `git fetch --prune origin` showed `origin/develop` still at merged commit `7bddb5d46a`
  - `git diff --check` passed
  - `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard packages/chip` produced no untracked nonignored chip files

## Remaining RISC-V Work

The current honest RISC-V state is still blocked on real promotion evidence: qemu/grub/agent-live evidence for the Debian rv64 image, generated-AP Linux boot beyond banner-only, E1 platform contract vs Chipyard ABI reconciliation, and target-side `/dev/e1-npu` smoke transcripts. This PR removes local source-check friction, prevents tracked evidence churn, and gets the chip Docker lint/typecheck/RTL/cocotb/dashboard/evidence-regression gates clean while those larger proofs are collected.

topPRs.title: feat(mobile): add local voice and sandbox routing
topPRs.author: Dexploarer
topPRs.body:
# Relates to - Follows the architecture work from #7779. - Accounts for #7844: upstream is collapsing the old Satellite/Carrot vocabulary into remote-mode Plugins (`mode: "direct" | "remote"`). This branch accepts the landed remote-plugin naming and keeps the implementation compatible with that direction. # Background ## What does this PR do? This PR makes the iOS app and remote coding path usable from mobile while preserving the new remote-plugin architecture boundary. - Adds the iOS local voice/onboarding path around the full Bun/native local-inference lane. - Moves mobile onboarding toward a simpler cloud/sky visual treatment with fewer cards, smaller radii, and clearer voice/download state. - Uses Kokoro/mobile TTS for onboarding greeting instead of blocking first-run on OmniVoice warmup. - Keeps local voice asset readiness separate from engine readiness so the UI does not claim synth success before a real probe works. - Carries the remote coding/sandbox work so mobile can route coding sessions to Eliza Cloud or home/remote workers instead of trying to run Codex/Claude/OpenCode/PTYs inside the iOS App Store client. - Keeps `plugin-coding-tools` parity scoped: `ls` can route through `eliza.fs`; grep/glob remain plugin-owned until explicit search/glob semantics exist; edit remains plugin-owned until a first-class patch/edit capability exists. - Reconciles the branch with latest `origin/develop`, including the Babylon -> Feed rename and the upstream remote-plugin vocabulary changes. ## Why is this needed? The product goal is that a user can open Eliza on a phone and still use the agent to talk, set up local voice/model state, sign into cloud, and start real coding work from any device. iOS cannot safely or App-Store-compatibly run arbitrary PTYs, Codex/Claude/OpenCode agent runners, or native build tools inside the client. Those jobs need to route to an Eliza Cloud sandbox or a trusted home/remote worker, while the mobile app stays the controller and renderer. This PR also removes ambiguity around voice readiness. Having the files on disk is not the same as having a working native synth engine, and onboarding should not pretend otherwise. # Risks Medium. This touches mobile onboarding, iOS local voice, capability routing tests, and the remote-plugin merge boundary. The riskiest areas are native iOS voice/runtime behavior on physical devices and CI paths that moved under the recent `develop` rename wave. # Documentation changes needed? Docs are included where this branch already introduced or updated the remote coding/sandbox and mobile paths. No separate migration guide is required for app users in this PR. # Testing ## Local validation run after reconciling `origin/develop` - `bun run test:remote-capabilities:surface-audit` - `ELIZAOS_STATIC_SOURCE_ONLY=1 ./scripts/static-smoke.sh` from `packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails` - `bun run --cwd packages/ui typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun typecheck` - `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun test` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-capacitor-bridge typecheck` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-coding-tools typecheck` - `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-coding-tools test src/actions/ls.test.ts src/actions/bash.test.ts --reporter verbose` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/kernel-patches/cpu-simd-kernels.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/kernel-patches/cpu-polar-kernels.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/build-llama-cpp-dflash.mjs` - `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/run-mobile-build.mjs` - `node --check packages/test/cloud-e2e/src/fixtures/stack.ts` - `node --check packages/tui/src/utils.ts` - `git diff --check` - `git diff --cached --check` ## Reviewer start point Start with: 1. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx` 2. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixSteps.tsx` 3. `packages/app-core/scripts/build-llama-cpp-dflash.mjs` 4. `plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/ls.ts` 5. Remote sandbox/cloud/home-worker docs and routing files touched by this branch # Deploy notes CI is rerunning on the pushed merge commit. #7844 is still open and dirty; this PR preserves compatibility with its stated direction without depending on it landing first.

Greptile Summary

 This PR wires up the iOS local-voice onboarding path, migrates remote sandbox/coding routing from \"satellite/carrot\" to a \"remote-plugin\" vocabulary, and adds a direct `invokeWorker`/`tailWorkerEvents` API to the `RemotePluginHost`. The UI onboarding states are removed in favour of a refactored `VoicePrefixSteps` component, and `ls` in `plugin-coding-tools` gains a capability-router fast path so mobile can delegate filesystem listings to Eliza Cloud. - **Vocabulary rename** – `E2BSatelliteCapabilityRouterService` and >40 env-variable names renamed; URL settings keep backward-compat fallbacks. - **`RemotePluginHost` extended** – new `invokeWorker`, `tailWorkerEvents`, and ring-buffered event log; service singletons in `rpc-handlers.ts` moved to module-level. - **`ls` routing** – capability-router fast path with local-FS fallback on `CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE`.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge; all findings are naming or type-strictness nits with no runtime impact. The core logic changes are all correct. The four flagged items are an env-variable name using old vocabulary, a redundant wire-type field, its mirror in the RPC schema, and a loosened TypeScript index-signature that could mask future mutation bugs but has no runtime consequence today. packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts and packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-schema.ts carry the redundant satelliteId field and old-vocabulary env var.

Important Files Changed

 | Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts | Adds invokeWorker, tailWorkerEvents, ring-buffered event log, and three new host injection points; ELIZA_CARROT_MAX_WORKER_EVENTS uses old vocabulary and RemotePluginWorkerEventRecord carries a redundant satelliteId field. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-schema.ts | Adds RemotePluginWorkerEventRecord and RPC entries for dynamic views, trace, voice, database, and launch; satelliteId field is redundant with remotePluginId. | | packages/agent/src/services/e2b-capability-router.ts | Large mechanical rename from satellite/carrot to remote vocabulary; URL-based settings keep backward-compat fallbacks; logic unchanged. | | plugins/plugin-coding-tools/src/actions/ls.ts | Adds capability-router fast path; falls back to local FS on CAPABILITY_UNAVAILABLE; helper functions extracted cleanly. | | packages/ui/src/api/client-voice-profiles.ts | normaliseCaptureSession now returns a local fallback without server sessionId when prompts fail validation, correctly preventing mismatched audio uploads. | | packages/plugin-remote-manifest/src/types.ts | JsonObject index signature changed from readonly to mutable; new HostRequestMethod entries added. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/rpc-handlers.ts | VoiceService and LaunchOrchestrator moved to module-level singletons; large set of new RPC handlers wired up. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx`, line 523-537 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/a3c58597f4fa5ab5a11d3acce066ffea612c2a7c/packages/ui/src/components/onboarding/VoicePrefixGate.tsx#L523-L537)) **useEffect dependency declared but never called inside the effect** `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness` is listed in the dependency array, but the effect body never invokes it — it defines its own inner `refresh` closure that calls `loadVoiceBundleReadiness` directly. Because `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness` is stable (empty `useCallback` deps), this doesn't cause extra re-runs, but the dependency is misleading and the `alive` guard inside the effect duplicates logic already present in `refreshVoiceBundleReadiness`.
Reviews (4): Last reviewed commit: ["fix: update capability naming audit self..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/4c6966c0e505173d6f8c92159068bbb86127667b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33064449)

topPRs.title: fix: stabilize gpt-oss routing and opencode handoffs
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary This PR stabilizes the gpt-oss/OpenCode live path by tightening the generic planner/tool contracts rather than adding deployment-specific prompt hacks. It includes: - Stabilized `TASKS` / OpenCode app handoff behavior so completed sub-agent app builds return clean final URLs instead of visible thread/progress spam, stale attachment leakage, duplicate finals, or session/cancel noise. - Preserved Discord connector reply references so final bot messages stay attached to the triggering prompt. - Added malformed Stage 1 tool-call recovery for model outputs that contain valid tool calls in less-than-ideal response shapes. - Throttled and deduped autonomy/batch-drain task loops so repeated persisted drain tasks do not flood trajectories. - Preserved a single successful tool's explicit `userFacingText` over evaluator paraphrase, preventing exact paths/numbers from being hallucinated during final synthesis. ## Why Live gpt-oss-120b + OpenCode testing exposed several real issues in Discord trajectories: - App builds could create visible task-thread / emoji progress noise even though the sub-agent finished successfully. - Planner/action routing could recover from bad model shapes but needed a generic retry path. - Autonomy drain tasks could duplicate and produce noisy repeated trajectories. - Final response synthesis could paraphrase exact tool output and mutate factual strings, e.g. `/home/milady/.bun` became `/home/milody/.bun` even though the shell tool returned the correct verified `userFacingText`. ## Validation Focused code validation on latest `origin/develop`: - `packages/core`: focused suite `164/164`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator`: focused suite `150/150`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-coding-tools`: `bun test --timeout 10000 src/actions/bash.test.ts` -> `38/38`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-sql`: `bun test src/__tests__/integration/task.real.test.ts` -> `5/5`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` clean Live Discord/API/log/trajectory validation after restart from local source: - Disk/RAM prompt `1506974790538362920` -> reply `1506974985942601839`, trajectory `tj-310e968b11fa94` - `SHELL`, 1 tool call, 0 tool failures, 0 evaluator failures - no Discord thread, no reactions/progress markers - verified final path stayed `/home/milady/.bun`; no `/home/milody` typo - BTC prompt `1506973638878957578` -> reply `1506973663180886127`, trajectory `tj-2cdd689447708e` - `SHELL`, 1 tool call, 0 tool failures, 0 evaluator failures - App-build prompt `1506973704100380755` -> reply `1506973786526711839`, trajectories `tj-2d1a1d8b231a54` and `tj-2d5c9fe64d849e` - `TASKS` / OpenCode path, 0 tool failures - no visible Discord thread, no reactions/progress markers - final URL: https://nubilio.org/apps/micro-journal-prompt/ (HTTP 200 verified) Evidence JSON is saved locally under: - `/home/milady/session-summary/live-clean-opencode-20260521-final/` - `/home/milady/session-summary/live-clean-opencode-20260521-preserve/` ## Notes This intentionally does not include the rejected regex-based safety detour from local testing. That approach overblocked unrelated disk/app prompts and was removed. Any regulated-commerce policy work should be handled separately through the normal safety/policy layer, not hard-coded routing regexes in this PR.

Greptile Summary

This PR stabilizes the gpt-oss/OpenCode live path by tightening several planner and routing contracts: it adds malformed Stage 1 tool-call recovery, throttles/deduplicates autonomy drain tasks, preserves a single tool's exact `userFacingText` over evaluator paraphrase, adds configurable sub-agent progress policy (compact/threaded/silent with a startup delay), and restores Discord reply references for TASKS-spawned bot messages. - **Core planner changes** (`planner-loop.ts`, `action-tiering.ts`, `message.ts`): `preferredFinalMessageFromToolOrModel` now prefers a single successful tool's `userFacingText` over the evaluator's synthesized reply; Stage 1 retry logic is extended to catch malformed (present-but-empty) `HANDLE_RESPONSE` tool calls; canonical action names are registered before similes to prevent simile-based capture of real action surfaces. - **Orchestrator + Discord** (`plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/index.ts`, `plugin-discord/service.ts`): Sub-agent progress delivery is gated by a configurable delay and mode policy, clearing timers on `task_complete`/`task_error`; Discord reply references are now propagated through `originConnectorMessageId` so final bot messages reply to the triggering prompt; `sub-agent-router.ts` adds verify-retry event suppression and makes `requireFresh` opt-in so files pre-dating a session start are no longer treated as dead URLs. - **Infrastructure** (`task-drain.ts`, `batcher.ts`, `dispatcher.ts`, `plugin-sql/base.ts`): Duplicate persisted drain tasks are deleted on startup; recurring batcher sections are throttled by `minCycleMs`; `agentId` is added to `getTasksByName`/`getTask` return shapes needed by `matchesTask`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge with minor cleanup; all changes are well-tested and address real live-trajectory issues observed in production. The three flagged issues are all non-blocking: a dead `chunks.length === 1` condition in the Discord multi-chunk path (doesn't affect the final reply behavior), a `shouldRun` predicate evaluated after the recurring-section timestamp is already advanced (potential over-suppression in niche section configs), and a redundant `typeof serialized === "string"` guard in `isUnsupportedCancelError`. None introduce incorrect behavior in the primary live paths. The core behavioral changes — Stage 1 retry extension, tool-text priority, action-tiering canonical-first registration, delay-based progress gating, and verify-retry suppression — are all clearly intentional, consistently tested, and address concrete trajectory failures observed in live runs. `plugins/plugin-discord/service.ts` for the dead condition in the multi-chunk send path; `packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts` for the mark-before-shouldRun ordering.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/runtime/planner-loop.ts | Adds `preferredFinalMessageFromToolOrModel` that gives a single successful tool's `userFacingText` priority over the evaluator's paraphrase to prevent hallucination of exact paths/values; logic is correct and well-covered by the new happy-path test. | | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Extends the Stage 1 empty-completion retry to also detect and retry malformed `HANDLE_RESPONSE` tool calls (tool present but arguments unparseable); retry limit and logging are correctly updated, and the new test covers the recovery path. | | packages/core/src/features/autonomy/service.ts | Restricts broad participant-room scanning to an explicit opt-in flag (`AUTONOMY_INCLUDE_ALL_ROOMS=true`), preventing inadvertent room enumeration; hoists autonomy memory fetch before the room-ID check so thought context is always included. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/index.ts | Large addition of configurable progress policy (`compact`/`threaded`/`silent`) and a timer-based delay mechanism that suppresses early sub-agent progress noise; delay logic is largely correct but a narrow race where the timer fires just as `task_complete` is processed can emit a stale progress message after the completion one. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts | Adds session-event suppression after a verify-retry handoff and filters internal `session/cancel` MCP errors; changes `requireFresh` from opt-out (`!== false`) to opt-in (`=== true`), intentionally allowing pre-session-start files to pass verification, which the new stale-mtime test validates. | | plugins/plugin-discord/service.ts | Adds Discord reply-reference propagation via `discordReplyReferenceFromContent`; single-chunk path is correct and tested, but the multi-chunk path contains a dead `chunks.length === 1` condition that can never be true inside `if (chunks.length > 1)`. | | packages/core/src/utils/batch-queue/task-drain.ts | Deduplicates stale persisted drain tasks on startup by deleting all but the first matching task; uses `Promise.allSettled` so a single delete failure won't abort the rest. | | packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts | Adds `minCycleMs` throttle for recurring sections via `_markRecurringSectionAttempt`; timestamp is marked before `shouldRun` is evaluated, which could cause over-suppression when `shouldRun` gates a section that becomes ready within the cycle window. | | plugins/plugin-sql/src/base.ts | Adds the missing `agentId` field to `getTasksByName` and `getTask` return mappings, needed by `TaskDrain.matchesTask` to filter tasks by agent; simple and targeted fix. | | packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/dispatcher.ts | Passes the maximum `maxRetries` across a call-plan's sections into the model invocation options; straightforward and well-tested by the new dispatcher test. | | packages/core/src/runtime/action-tiering.ts | Refactors candidate matching to build a canonical-owners map so real action names take priority over simile-based matches, preventing a simile of `TASKS` from capturing the `TASKS` action surface. | | packages/core/src/features/basic-capabilities/providers/attachments.ts | Skips attachment prompt rendering for `sub_agent` source messages, preventing stale sub-agent progress attachments from leaking into subsequent prompt context. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User (Discord) participant DS as DiscordService participant MH as MessageHandler (Stage1) participant PL as PlannerLoop participant OR as OrchestratorPlugin participant SA as SubAgent (OpenCode) participant SR as SubAgentRouter U->>DS: Send prompt message DS->>MH: runV5MessageRuntimeStage1() MH-->>MH: Stage1 retry if empty/malformed tool call MH->>PL: planned_reply (TASKS action) PL->>OR: TASKS action handler OR->>SA: spawn sub-agent session OR-->>DS: (no progress yet, within delayMs) SA-->>OR: progress events OR-->>DS: emitProgress after delayMs (compact text) SA-->>SR: task_complete event SR->>SR: verifyMappedLocalUrl (requireFresh opt-in) SR-->>OR: clearDelayedProgress(sessionId) Note over PL: Single tool userFacingText wins over evaluator paraphrase PL->>DS: finalMessage with originConnectorMessageId DS->>U: Reply referencing original prompt message ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts`, line 698-710 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/4ba5130529b67ab5c621281f6aa397fe106759f4/packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts#L698-L710)) `_markRecurringSectionAttempt` is called before the `shouldRun` predicate is evaluated. If `shouldRun` returns `false` (e.g., "no pending work yet"), the timestamp is still advanced, so the section will be suppressed for the full `minCycleMs` window even though it never produced any model output. For sections that combine `shouldRun` with a long `minCycleMs`, this can delay the first actual execution if the predicate flips to `true` shortly after a skipped cycle.
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(core): preserve single-tool user-fac..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/4ba5130529b67ab5c621281f6aa397fe106759f4) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33219863) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: fix(os-rv64): keep release gates source-only honest
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- wire the unified `packages/os/linux/elizaos` RV64 release-check targets to the checked-in Python manifest gate instead of the deleted `scripts/release-check.sh`
- keep the checked-in RV64 release manifest source-only honest: `planned` artifact metadata with missing evidence rows until a build archives the ISO plus qemu/GRUB/agent transcripts
- make static smoke catch stale release-check target wiring without requiring a local ISO or QEMU run
- make the ISO build fail closed if `manifest.json.template` is missing, so it cannot emit an ISO/checksum without release metadata
- keep the prototype dashboard conservative for source-only checkouts while still allowing generated NPU evidence to satisfy the validator when present

## Why

After the merged RISC-V cleanup, the active Linux tree is `packages/os/linux/elizaos`, not the removed `elizaos-debian-riscv64` variant. The source tree still had two release-gate drift points: the Makefile called a deleted helper, and the committed RV64 manifest claimed collected qemu/GRUB/agent evidence even though the transcript and ISO are not archived in the repo. That made source-only validation either miss the stale helper path or fail for the wrong reason.

This PR keeps the claim boundary explicit: the checked-in manifest is a planned skeleton, default `release-check` is informational/BLOCKED, strict release-check remains nonzero until real release evidence is present, and qemu-virt summary evidence is not treated as promoted release evidence without the matching transcript/artifact.

## Validation

- `bash -n packages/os/linux/elizaos/build.sh`: pass
- `ELIZAOS_STATIC_SOURCE_ONLY=1 ./scripts/static-smoke.sh` from `packages/os/linux/elizaos`: pass
- `make -C packages/os/linux/elizaos release-check ARCH=riscv64`: exits 0 with `STATUS: BLOCKED`
- `make -C packages/os/linux/elizaos release-check-strict ARCH=riscv64`: exits nonzero with `STATUS: BLOCKED`
- `make -C packages/os/linux/elizaos release-check ARCH=amd64`: refuses the RISC-V-specific gate
- `make -C packages/chip prototype-status-dashboard-check`: pass
- `make -C packages/chip chip-os-objective-evidence-matrix`: blocked as expected, `requirements=17 proven=0 blocked=3 missing=14`
- `make -C packages/chip chip-os-report-freshness-test`: pass
- `python3 scripts/test_check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`: pass
- `python3 scripts/test_os_rv64_chip_boot_contract.py`: pass
- `python3 scripts/test_chip_os_report_freshness.py`: pass
- `git diff --check`: pass

## Not Claimed

- no E1/chip Linux boot claim
- no promoted RV64 release artifact claim
- no qemu-virt evidence substitution for generated Eliza AP/chip-emulator evidence
- no hardware, silicon, phone, AOSP, or performance readiness claim

topPRs.title: feat(cloud): Hetzner control plane IaC + data plane naming + legacy milady-core deprecation
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## Summary Three coordinated changes that move the Hetzner setup from "manually-poked VMs" to a proper two-tier architecture: 1. **Terraform IaC for the control plane**: declares the persistent VM(s) that host the orchestrator daemon (`provisioning-worker`, `agent-router`, `headscale`, `cloudflared`). Uses `hetznercloud/hcloud` + `cloudflare` providers, Cloudflare R2 as S3 state backend. Includes cloud-init bootstrap, tfvars examples, and a README walkthrough for `terraform import` of the existing prod VM (`89.167.63.246`). 2. **Data-plane naming**: `node-` becomes `eliza-core-` going forward. `generateNodeId()` now sources entropy from `crypto.getRandomValues()` instead of `Math.random().toString(16).slice(2, 10)` — the latter silently strips trailing zeros and could produce short or colliding suffixes when `node_id` is UNIQUE in `docker_nodes`. 3. **Data-plane location default fixed**: Hetzner deprecated `cpx32` on `ash` (Ashburn), so `defaultHcloudLocation = "ash"` failed with "unsupported location for server type". Flipped to `fsn1` to match the actual prod fleet. 4. **Migration 0132**: disables the 6 legacy `milady-core-*` rows (`enabled=false`, `capacity=8`). They were inserted by hand in 2026-03 with `capacity=100` (unrealistic for cpx32), have been health-check offline for weeks, and are now ignored by the autoscaler. Existing sandboxes keep running on the underlying Docker daemons until their next user-triggered restart, at which point the daemon provisions a replacement on a fresh autoscaled core. Ops follow-up (delete Hetzner servers + DB rows) is documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md`. `packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/ARCHITECTURE.md` formalises the two-tier model (static control plane vs elastic data plane) so future ops actions have a clear runbook. ## What this PR does NOT do (followups) - Terraform for headscale state + the cloudflared tunnel - `terraform-apply` GitHub workflow - Migrating the 4 remaining cron paths (`pool-replenish`, `pool-health-check`, `pool-image-rollout`, `deployment-monitor`) off the orphan `container-control-plane` service onto the daemon-queue pattern, then retiring the service entirely - Raising the Hetzner Cloud server-count limit (ops ticket) ## Test plan - [x] `bun test packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/containers/node-autoscaler.test.ts` — 5/5 pass (3 existing + 2 new for the `generateNodeId()` rename and entropy fix) - [x] `bunx tsc --noEmit` on `packages/cloud-shared` — clean (pre-existing core/shared noise unrelated) - [x] `terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate` on `packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/` — success - [x] `terraform fmt -recursive -check` — clean - [x] R2 backend bucket `eliza-terraform-state` exists in WEUR (verified) - [ ] After merge: import the existing prod VM into Terraform state via the steps in the README - [ ] After merge + Hetzner limit raise: verify a fresh `eliza-core-` provisions via autoscale and milady-core-* sandboxes drain naturally on restart ## Ran the `/clean` skill on this PR - Agent 1 (Slop & Larp): inlined a trivial `modules/manager-vm/` wrapper, removed a dead-exported helper (-87 LOC) - Agent 2 (Types & Structure): clean, surfaced the `ash` vs `fsn1` inconsistency — fixed in this PR - Agent 3 (Dead Code & Legacy): no leftovers - Agent 4 (Defensive → Clean): caught the `Math.random()` slicing bug, replaced with `crypto.getRandomValues` - Agent 5 (Tests & DRY): added the 2 missing tests for `generateNodeId()` ## Out-of-band ops actions needed for production rollout 1. Generate R2 API token (already done — bucket `eliza-terraform-state` exists) 2. Set `HETZNER_CLOUD_API_KEY` + `CONTAINERS_AUTOSCALE_PUBLIC_SSH_KEY` on the daemon (already done on staging VM at 89.167.63.246) 3. Open Hetzner ticket to raise the server-count limit past 10 so autoscale can actually create replacement cores

Greptile Summary

This PR transitions the Hetzner setup to a two-tier architecture with Terraform IaC for the static control plane, fixes the `defaultHcloudLocation` fallback from the deprecated `ash` to `fsn1`, replaces `Math.random()`-based node ID generation with `crypto.getRandomValues()`, renames node IDs from `node-` to `eliza-core-`, and disables the six legacy `milady-core-*` DB rows via migration 0132. - **Terraform control plane**: New `hetzner/control-plane/` module declares Hetzner VMs + Cloudflare DNS records backed by Cloudflare R2 state; cloud-init template handles first-boot setup of Docker, Bun, and the deploy user. - **`generateNodeId()` fix**: 4 bytes from `crypto.getRandomValues()` hex-encoded with `padStart` guarantees exactly 8 hex characters, preventing the trailing-zero truncation bug in the previous `Math.random().toString(16).slice(2,10)` path. - **Migration 0132**: Flips all `milady-core-*` rows to `enabled=false, capacity=8` so the autoscaler ignores them while live sandboxes drain naturally on their next restart.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The TypeScript and migration changes are safe to merge, but the Terraform module has two gaps that would prevent a usable deployment: no guard against an empty SSH key list (produces a permanently inaccessible VM) and no authorized_keys injection for the deploy user (blocks the GitHub Actions deploy workflow). The node-autoscaler entropy fix and the milady-core migration are clean and well-tested. The defaultHcloudLocation fix is a straightforward one-liner. The risk sits entirely in the new Terraform module: applying with the default empty ssh_public_keys creates a VM nobody can access, and the deploy user created by cloud-init has no SSH authorized keys so the expected deploy workflow cannot SSH in. variables.tf (missing SSH key validation) and cloud-init/bootstrap.yaml.tftpl (missing ssh_authorized_keys for the deploy user) need attention before the module is run against any environment.

Security Review

 - **Unverified installer scripts in cloud-init** (`bootstrap.yaml.tftpl` lines 49–53): both Docker (`curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh`) and Bun (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`) are fetched and executed without checksum verification. The control-plane VM holds `DATABASE_URL`, `HCLOUD_TOKEN`, Headscale state, and the cloudflared tunnel — a supply-chain or MITM attack at bootstrap time would silently compromise the entire control plane.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/variables.tf | Defines all Terraform input variables; ssh_public_keys defaults to [] with no minimum-length validation, allowing a zero-key apply that produces an inaccessible VM. | | packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/cloud-init/bootstrap.yaml.tftpl | Cloud-init bootstrap template: creates deploy user but injects no SSH authorized keys for that user, blocking the GitHub Actions deploy workflow. Uses unverified curl|sh for Docker and Bun installs on the most sensitive VM in the fleet. | | packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/main.tf | Core Terraform resources for control-plane VMs and Cloudflare DNS records. SSH key resources use positional list indexing which causes unnecessary plan churn on key reorder. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/containers/node-autoscaler.ts | Replaces Math.random().toString(16).slice(2,10) with crypto.getRandomValues() over 4 bytes; renames prefix from node- to eliza-core-. Correct and well-tested. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/containers/node-autoscaler.test.ts | Adds two new tests: validates eliza-core-[0-9a-f]{8} format and uniqueness across 50 consecutive provisions. Both correct. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/migrations/0132_legacy_milady_cores_disable.sql | Sets enabled=false, capacity=8 for all milady-core-* rows. SQL correct; WHERE clause appropriately scoped; journal idx:131 matches the 0-based convention. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/config/containers-env.ts | Changes defaultHcloudLocation fallback from ash to fsn1, fixing provisioning failures after Hetzner deprecated cpx32 on ash. |

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/variables.tf`, line 536-540 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/3a896d4d3db25e737e69fca00f2f5a7a72db5aff/packages/cloud-infra/cloud/terraform/hetzner/control-plane/variables.tf#L536-L540)) **No SSH key validation — VM becomes inaccessible on `terraform apply`** `var.ssh_public_keys` defaults to `[]`, and there is no `validation` block requiring at least one entry. When Hetzner creates a server, it injects the listed SSH public keys into root's `~/.ssh/authorized_keys`. With an empty list, the VM boots with no authorized key for root, and the `deploy` user also has no keys (see the cloud-init template). The only recovery is Hetzner rescue mode.
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["feat(cloud): Hetzner control plane IaC +..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/3a896d4d3db25e737e69fca00f2f5a7a72db5aff) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33367829) > Greptile also left **3 inline comments** on this PR.
